(4) Ro Khanna CA-17 — Hindu (Gandhian); Heritage Action 119th 0% / lifetime 8-10%; Vice Chair Progressive Caucus; PP Mar Monte board 2006-2013; cosponsor WHPA + Equality Act; YES Respect for Marriage Roll Call 373; set.office: U.S. Representative CA-14 (2026 D Candidate · Swalwell-vacancy · LOST June 2 primary 5th-of-9 w/ 2.67% · San Leandro Councilor D3)

profile.background: Victor Aguilar Jr., elected to San Leandro City Council D3 in 2018 (sworn-in early 2019), term through Dec 31, 2026; previously Vice Mayor (2021, 7-0 nomination); SLUSD School Board Member 2016-2018. BA Political Science, Hawaii Pacific University (2003); AA Liberal Studies, Pasadena City College; USC Local Leaders Program certificate (2019). Account Executive at Construction Testing Services (council bio). Married to Dr. Nolan Quinabo, PT, DPT. First openly gay San Leandro Councilmember; self-described '1st LatinX gay Vice Mayor.' Three bulldogs, Floresta Gardens neighborhood. Alameda County Democratic Central Committee Exec Committee; East Bay Stonewall Democratic Club Board; 2023 President National League of Cities LGBTQ+ Local Officials Committee.

victor-aguilar-jr.notes_set: STATUS FIX: LOST CA-14 June 2, 2026 D primary 5th-of-9 with 2.67% (Wahab 34.30% + Hernandez 16.09% advanced to November); did NOT advance to special-election seat. Remains sitting San Leandro Councilor D3 through Dec 2026. HARD-evidence catches all D-direction: (a) biblical_marriage q0/q1/q2/q4 FALSE via 5-source LGBT stack — first openly gay SL Councilor + married to Dr. Quinabo + 2022 Equality California endorsement + East Bay Stonewall Dem Club board + 2023 unanimous-elected NLC LGBTQ+ Local Officials Committee President + author of San Leandro's first Pride proclamation; (b) border_immigration q1/q2 FALSE via own campaign words ('protect our neighbors in the community from deportation' — Pleasanton Weekly forum) + sits on SL council w/ formal sanctuary policy (no 287(g), no ICE cooperation). NULL on life / family-ed / christian liberty / election / 2A / foreign-policy specifics: zero on-record campaign-Issues abortion stance (skipped April 2026 forum + did not respond KQED voter guide); no education policy beyond serving SLUSD board 2016-2018 without scored votes surfacing; no 2A statement (anti-police-militarization re: armored vehicle is not 2A-cell adjacent). Foreign-policy: supported Dec 2023 Gaza ceasefire resolution (withdrawn before formal vote) — not a clean fit to forever-wars/AIPAC cells.

0.note: First openly gay San Leandro Councilmember; married to Dr. Nolan Quinabo, PT, DPT (a man) — same-sex marriage in lived practice is opposite of affirming 1m1w-lifelong-union-as-instituted-by-God.

1.note: Self in a same-sex marriage + 2022 Equality California endorsement — opposite of opposing SSM/civil-unions/DPs in law. HARD.

2.note: Unanimously elected 2023 President of NLC LGBTQ+ Local Officials Committee (full LGBTQ+ remit incl. T) + East Bay Stonewall Democratic Club board (LGBTQ+ Dem caucus) — opposite of rejecting transgender ideology. HARD.

4.note: Authored / sponsored San Leandro's first Pride proclamation (unanimously approved) + 2023 NLC LGBTQ+ Local Officials Committee President — opposite of opposing LGBTQ+ promotion in public policy / schools. HARD.

1.note: Campaign platform explicitly pledges to 'protect our neighbors in the community from deportation' (Pleasanton Weekly Apr 2026 forum coverage) — opposite of mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens. HARD.

2.note: Sits on San Leandro Council which maintains formal sanctuary-city policies (no 287(g), no SLPD-ICE cooperation, no inquiry into immigration status); no public dissent on record from Aguilar + his own platform protects from deportation — opposite of opposing sanctuary policies. HARD.

set.office: U.S. Representative CA-15 (sworn in Jan 3, 2023 · 2026 reelection · ex-CA Assembly Speaker pro Tem 2014-2022)

Junipero Serra HS (Catholic, 1988); BA Communications USF (1992); MPA SF State (1998); Harvard Kennedy School exec leadership (2003). Founder KM2 Communications; district director to State Sen. Jackie Speier. South SF City Council 2007 (one year as Mayor); CA State Assembly 22nd District 2012-2022 (Speaker pro Tempore 2014-2022, 8 years presiding officer). 119th committee: Energy & Commerce.

set.office: U.S. Representative CA-16 (sworn in Jan 3, 2025 · 2026 reelection · ex-San Jose Mayor 2015-2022 · former federal prosecutor)

profile.religion: Roman Catholic (cafeteria-Catholic / publicly pro-choice on policy)
profile.background: Samuel Theodore Liccardo, born April 16, 1970, Saratoga CA (Sicilian/Irish/Mexican Californio). Married Jessica Garcia-Kohl 2013. BA Georgetown (Government, magna cum laude, Phi Beta Kappa); JD Harvard; MPP Harvard Kennedy School. Former federal prosecutor (Santa Clara County DA sex-crimes/child-exploitation; DOJ Civil Rights Division). San Jose City Council 2007-2014 (D3); Mayor of San Jose 2015 to Jan 2023. 119th committee: Financial Services (Digital Assets/FinTech/AI, National Security/Illicit Finance, Oversight & Investigations subcommittees). Caucuses: Congressional Equality, Problem Solvers, New Democratic Coalition, Congressional Hispanic Caucus, CAPAC, Ukraine Caucus.

sam-liccardo.notes_set: Heritage Action 119th 0% (lifetime 0%) — orthodox center-left technocrat with strong anti-2A bona fides. Catholic-but-pro-choice on the public record ('Catholic teachings informed his family's personal choices on abortion, every person must follow her own God-given conscience, and the law must allow every person to do just that'). HARD-FALSE life: NAY on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act HR 21 Roll Call 27 Jan 23 2025 (only D YES was Cuellar) + Planned Parenthood Action Fund 2024 primary endorsement + as Mayor proposed and Council approved $250K San Jose grant to Planned Parenthood Mar Monte post-Dobbs (largest such grant in city history). HARD-FALSE marriage: Cosponsor Equality Act HR 15 + Congressional Equality Caucus member + Equality California endorsement. HARD-FALSE family/ed: pro-public-education platform ('bolster public education') + Equality Act SOGI in education. HARD-FALSE border: NAY on ICE/DHS funding extension w/ press release ('ICE has abandoned its mission of removing violent criminals in favor of detaining children, shooting Americans, and terrorizing our communities') + defended San Jose sanctuary publicly to Trump 1.0 ('San Jose Welcomes Migrants'). HARD-FALSE self-defense: as Mayor authored first-in-nation San Jose gun-owner liability-insurance + annual fee ordinance Jan 2022 (worked w/ Brady United / Everytown / Giffords legal teams; city prevailed in federal court Aug 2022) + Everytown for Gun Safety Action Fund 2026 first-round endorsement + ghost-gun / straw-purchase restrictions as mayor + 2021 CNN op-ed supporting federal red-flag + UBC expansion. HARD-FALSE foreign-policy q1: supports continued aid to Ukraine, Israel, and Mexican border per Palo Alto Daily Post — not a restraint-coalition member. Heterodox-D wedges noted: (a) sanctuary violent-felon ICE-notification carve-out as mayor after 2019 Bambi Larson killing (rejected by Santa Clara BOS); (b) Israel arms conditionality + Block the Bombs Act HR 3565 cosponsor; (c) 'Hell No' on OBBBA HR 1 with $3.9T deficit framing; (d) bipartisan OPT-codification w/ Obernolte (R); (e) crypto-trading conflict-of-interest restrictions for Members.

set.office: U.S. Representative CA-17 (sworn in Jan 3, 2017 · 5 terms · 2026 reelection · Vice Chair + Deputy Whip Progressive Caucus)

profile.background: Rohit 'Ro' Khanna, born Sept 13, 1976, Philadelphia (Indian Punjabi family). BA Economics, U. Chicago (Phi Beta Kappa); JD Yale Law. Visiting lecturer economics, Stanford 2012-2016. Deputy Assistant Secretary, U.S. Dept. of Commerce, 2009-2011 (Obama admin). Wife Ritu Ahuja; two children. Board member, Planned Parenthood Mar Monte 2006-2013. Vice Chair + Deputy Whip Congressional Progressive Caucus; Ranking Member House Select Committee on the CCP (119th); member LGBT Equality Caucus, Transgender Equality Task Force, House Antitrust Caucus. Founder No PAC Caucus. Widely speculated 2028 Democratic presidential contender.

ro-khanna.notes_set: Heritage Action 119th 0% / lifetime ~8-10% — orthodox progressive on culture-war axes but **three HARD CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS** in foreign-policy restraint that flip cells TRUE for him in a way no other CA D scorecard hits. HARD-FALSE life: cosponsor + YES on Women's Health Protection Act HR 8296 (117th) Roll Call 360 + YES HR 12 (119th) + NAY Born-Alive HR 26 Roll Call 29 (118th); SBA Pro-Life 0; NARAL/RFA 100%; PP Action Fund 100% + Khanna sat on PP Mar Monte board 2006-2013. HARD-FALSE marriage: YES on Respect for Marriage HR 8404 Roll Call 373 Dec 2022 + Equality Act cosponsor + Transgender Equality Task Force. HARD-FALSE family/ed q0/q2: NAY on Parents Bill of Rights HR 5 Roll Call 161 Mar 2023 + Equality Act SOGI in schools. HARD-FALSE election: YES Freedom to Vote/John Lewis VRA + HR 1 expansion of federal voting framework. HARD-FALSE border: NAY Secure the Border HR 2 Roll Call 209 + NAY Laken Riley HR 29 Roll Call 6 Jan 2025. HARD-FALSE self-defense q1: explicitly supports AR-15 ban + magazine limits + UBC. **HARD-TRUE foreign_policy_restraint q0 (Article I war powers): lead House sponsor of Yemen War Powers Resolution with Sanders 2019 — FIRST WPR ever to pass both chambers (Trump vetoed); also lead w/ Massie on Iran War Powers Resolution June 2025.** **HARD-TRUE q1 (forever wars / AUMF): consistent anti-forever-war voice + 2002 Iraq AUMF repeal supporter.** **HARD-TRUE q3 (no AIPAC/foreign-PAC $): Founder No PAC Caucus refusing all PAC + lobbyist + Super PAC + AIPAC money since first term; publicly demands DNC reject AIPAC ('Anyone who wants to lead this party must condemn and reject AIPAC money').** HARD-FALSE q4 (UN/supranational): voted to preserve UNRWA funding (NO on UNRWA-defund FY24 spending bill in protest of cut). **HARD-TRUE industry_capture q4: NAY on FY26 $838.7B defense appropriations bill — anti-MIC defense-contractor-accountability vote.** Heterodox-D: cosponsored bipartisan Khanna/Luna/Kaptur/Biggs bill codifying Trump's MFN drug-pricing executive order; 50-state federal-funds fraud audit bill w/ Burchett (R) Mar 2026.

0.note: YES on Women's Health Protection Act HR 8296 Roll Call 360 (Jul 15 2022) + cosponsor HR 12 (119th) + SBA Pro-Life 'consistently voted to eliminate protections for the unborn' + NARAL/RFA 100% — opposite of affirming life from conception. HARD.

2.note: Member Transgender Equality Task Force + LGBT Equality Caucus — opposite of rejecting transgender ideology. HARD.

4.note: Equality Act cosponsor + LGBT Equality Caucus member — opposite of opposing LGBTQ+ promotion in public policy. HARD.

2.note: Equality Act cosponsor codifies SOGI in K-12 education + LGBT Equality Caucus + Transgender Equality Task Force — opposite of opposing CRT/SOGI/comp-sex-ed in K-12. HARD.

1.note: YES on Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act HR 5746 (117th) which preempts state photo voter ID frameworks via federal floor — opposite of supporting photo voter ID with citizenship verification. HARD.

2.note: YES on HR 1 / Freedom to Vote / John Lewis Act expanding mail/early-voting federally — opposite of single-day in-person voting. HARD.

3.note: Supported HR 1 / Freedom to Vote which mandates mass mail-in / drop-box / no-excuse absentee — opposite of opposing mass mail-in. HARD.

Aligned w/ Article I congressional war-powers requirement. HARD-TRUE CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION.

NAY on FY26 $838.7B defense appropriations bill ('I just voted no on the FY26 $838.7 billion defense funding bill') — aligned with defense-contractor accountability / ending revolving-door appointments via opposing inflated defense procurement. HARD-TRUE cross-direction signal.

set.office: U.S. Representative CA-18 (sworn in Jan 3, 1995 · 16th term · 2026 reelection · Ranking Member House Science · ex-Chair House Admin 117th)

profile.religion: Lutheran (per Pew Faith on the Hill 117th roster)
profile.background: Susan Ellen 'Zoe' Lofgren, born Dec 21, 1947, San Mateo CA (age 78). BA Political Science Stanford (1970); JD Santa Clara Law (1975). Staffer for Rep. Don Edwards 1970-1978; worked on Nixon impeachment articles drafting as House Judiciary Committee staff 1973-1974 while in law school. San Jose immigration-law partner; San Jose City College board; Santa Clara County Board of Supervisors 1981-1994 (13 years). Only Member of Congress to participate in all three modern presidential impeachments (Nixon staff 1974; Clinton 1998 as member; Trump 2019/2021 as manager). Jan 6 Select Committee full member. 119th: Ranking Member House Science, Space, and Technology; House Judiciary; subcommittees on Courts/IP/Internet, Immigration Integrity, Administrative State/Antitrust.

zoe-lofgren.notes_set: Senior 16-term D-CA-18 Silicon Valley. Heritage Action 119th 9% / lifetime 10%; Inst Legislative Analysis pattern matches; NumbersUSA F- (career, 118th, 119th); NRA-PVF F. HARD-FALSE life: NARAL/Reproductive Freedom for All 100% + cosponsor WHPA HR 12 (119th) + cosponsor EACH Act + PP Action Fund 2022 endorsement; Roe overturn = 'bleak day' statement. HARD-FALSE family q2: Equality Act cosponsor codifies SOGI in education. HARD-FALSE election: lead shepherd HR 1 For the People Act + lead shepherd Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act (117th) as House Admin Chair. HARD-FALSE border: NumbersUSA F- + sponsor HR 1511/HR 4696 registry amnesty + NO on Mayorkas impeachment Feb 13 2024 ('complete waste of time') + cosponsor HR 3368 Born in the USA Act blocking Trump birthright-citizenship EO. HARD-FALSE self-defense: NRA-PVF F + co-author HR 4918 Advancing Gun Safety Technology Act (smart-guns) + Gun Records Restoration & Preservation Act cosponsor + opposed HR 38 Concealed Carry Reciprocity in Judiciary markup. **HARD-TRUE foreign_policy_restraint q0: YES on H.Con.Res.38 Iran War Powers Resolution June 3 2026 — 'war requires Congressional consent.'** CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION on Article I. Heterodox-D: vocally opposed AICOA / Open App Markets Act Big-Tech antitrust ('Democratic holdout') — anti-cartel-busting on tech, but rubric q2 specifically targets Big Ag (Bayer/Monsanto/Cargill) so this does not score directly.
